title: IntersectionObservers persist across Astro page loads without cleanup
reasoning: Each astro:page-load creates a new local IntersectionObserver and observes every .reveal element. The observer is only unregistered per element after that element intersects; elements that never intersect before an Astro navigation can remain observed, retaining detached DOM nodes and callback state across subsequent page loads. Over repeated client-side navigations this can accumulate observers and stale element references.
recommendation: Track the observer and pending timers at module scope and disconnect/clear them on the relevant Astro navigation cleanup event before initializing a new observer, or reuse a single observer and skip elements already initialized.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests were included for this feature, so there is no coverage exercising repeated astro:page-load events with non-intersecting reveal elements.
suggestedRegressionTest: In a DOM test, dispatch astro:page-load with a .reveal element that never intersects, then simulate a navigation cleanup/page-load cycle and assert the previous observer is disconnected or no stale element remains observed.

history.reasoning: The original evidence path still exists, but the cited evidence has changed. src/scripts/scroll-reveal.js now tracks activeObserver and pendingTimers at module scope, calls cleanupScrollReveal() at the start of every initScrollReveal(), disconnects the active observer, clears pending timers, and also registers cleanupScrollReveal for astro:before-swap. Current lines 4-13 define cleanup, lines 25-55 initialize/reuse state safely, and lines 57-58 wire Astro lifecycle events. A targeted DOM-stub reproduction dispatched astro:page-load twice with a non-intersecting .reveal element and verified the first observer was disconnected before the second page load; it also verified astro:before-swap disconnects the active observer. I found no committed scroll-reveal regression test. The repo-native npm test command could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vite/Vitest attempted to write node_modules/.vite-temp and failed with EPERM, so the conclusion is based on current code inspection plus the targeted executable reproduction.
commands: rg -n "IntersectionObserver|astro:page-load|astro:before|astro:after|scroll-reveal|\.reveal|initScrollReveal|disconnect\(|unobserve\(" src test tests spec specs .clawpatch 2>/dev/null

node --input-type=module -e "const listeners = new Map(); let currentReveals = []; const instances = []; class MockIntersectionObserver { constructor(callback, options) { this.callback = callback; this.options = options; this.observed = []; this.disconnectCount = 0; instances.push(this); } observe(el) { this.observed.push(el); } unobserve(el) { this.observed = this.observed.filter(item => item !== el); } disconnect() { this.disconnectCount += 1; this.observed = []; } } globalThis.IntersectionObserver = MockIntersectionObserver; globalThis.window = { IntersectionObserver: MockIntersectionObserver, matchMedia() { return { matches: false }; } }; globalThis.document = { addEventListener(type, handler) { listeners.set(type, handler); }, querySelectorAll(selector) { if (selector !== '.reveal') throw new Error('unexpected selector ' + selector); return currentReveals; } }; const makeReveal = name => ({ name, style: { getPropertyValue() { return '0'; } }, classList: { add() {} } }); await import('./src/scripts/scroll-reveal.js?observer-cleanup-revalidation=' + Date.now()); const load = listeners.get('astro:page-load'); const beforeSwap = listeners.get('astro:before-swap'); if (typeof load !== 'function') throw new Error('astro:page-load handler missing'); if (typeof beforeSwap !== 'function') throw new Error('astro:before-swap handler missing'); const first = makeReveal('first'); currentReveals = [first]; load(); if (instances.length !== 1 || instances[0].observed[0] !== first) throw new Error('first reveal was not observed'); const second = makeReveal('second'); currentReveals = [second]; load(); if (instances[0].disconnectCount !== 1 || instances[0].observed.length !== 0) throw new Error('previous observer was not disconnected before second page load'); if (instances.length !== 2 || instances[1].observed[0] !== second) throw new Error('second reveal was not observed by new observer'); beforeSwap(); if (instances[1].disconnectCount !== 1 || instances[1].observed.length !== 0) throw new Error('active observer was not disconnected before swap'); console.log('observer cleanup across Astro events passed');"
